message: Jake
Detective Maurice
Detective Cosner
Recently, there was a shocking incident where the severed ears of BAM editor Eva Courtney were delivered.
Since then, there have been concerns about Courtney's well-being, but it seems she's met a tragic end.
This morning, while jogging along the East River promenade, a man discovered a suspicious garbage bag in a corner of the park and reported it to the police.
As a result, dismembered remains of Courtney were found.
However, strangely enough, besides the severed ears, the torso was missing, which led to speculation that this might be the work of the infamous serial murder case.
Hoo.
So, Eva was found as a dismembered corpse. This morning's news is pretty hard.
The uproar over Eva's ear incident was only two days ago.
After being dismembered and with no torso, it seems this is actually the work of the killer.
Could it really be the actions of that murderer?
Look
Talk
Think
System
Surroundings
What has happened so far
Save
Exit the game
Thanks to clean-freak Yuriko, there's not a speck of dust on the desk. Is it because of her Japanese mother's influence that she's this neat?
Because of that, the already empty office looks even more spacious, and she even throws away DM and invoices without asking.
It sounds nice to say it's neatly organized, but the truth is, there's nothing here. It's proof that we aren't busy.
With the office already not spacious, having two detectives taking up a lot of space is a challenge.
Moreover, there’s only one exit, and that path is blocked, making escape impossible. This is the worst.
To break this situation,
I wonder if I should try jumping out the window? It’s just behind my chair.
He has the vibe of a uniformed cop climbing the ranks. That’s probably true.
Honestly, this bland middle-aged detective is more of a hassle than that tough young punk next to him.
Worn-out shirt and a tattered coat. You can’t see his shoes, but they’re probably worn down too.
From any angle, he looks like a detective. I can’t believe there’s still a fossil like him left in New York.
He has a gentle-looking face, but he’s probably seen plenty of bloody crime scenes.
He gives off a rich kid vibe from college, but he perfectly plays the heel role assigned to the baby-faced old detective.
He doesn’t seem like just a fool.
He’s probably quite talented if he’s been assigned to the homicide division.
Those broad shoulders.
A person like me is completely outmatched. Plus, that arrogance seems to suit him well. He might be cut out for being a detective.
Anyway, it’s probably better not to provoke him.
Have you watched the news recently?
Yeah, somewhat.
Then you must know about the murder of a woman named Eva Courtney.
Yeah. They talked about it on TV this morning.
To be frank, I’ve come here because a man pointed out that you might be the culprit.
Me, the culprit!? Who would say something so ridiculous?
Look, he’s flustered. This guy must be the culprit after all.
Is there anyone who wouldn't be shaken by suddenly being labeled a criminal? This is no joke; I'd like you to stop with the baseless accusations.
No, no, I don’t think you’re the criminal or anything. I just came to hear your side of the story, no hard feelings, okay?
When was the last time you saw Eva?
I think it was about a week ago. She came here.
Did you have an appointment?
No. She just showed up out of the blue, said what she wanted, and left.
What’s that supposed to mean? Answer me seriously. What did you talk about?
Well, she always just shows up unexpectedly, talks selfishly, and then heads off.
What did she talk about that time?
At that time, she was really excited saying she had a scoop on the serial murder case.
So, it was just you and Eva here at that moment?
No, my assistant, Yuriko Obrien, was here too. Unfortunately, she still hasn’t arrived today.
Lying won’t do you any good.
I’m not lying!
Cosner, knock it off.
So she really did say she had a scoop on the serial murder case?
Yes, that’s correct.
I’m glad I got to meet you today. Up until now, I hadn’t gotten much from this, so she has a scoop, huh?
Are you checking around with other people too?
Of course! Are you stupid or what?
Hey, watch your language.
I also visited Kristy Darn before.
To Kristy’s place? What does Eva’s death have to do with her?
Actually, someone mentioned your name and asked them to look into Kristy. But it seems they were completely off track.
Sorry for the ruckus since morning.
Well then, we’ll take our leave now. Thank you for your cooperation.
You did it, didn’t you?
Huh!?
Hey, Cosner, can you be quiet? I’ll be the one talking here, so just keep quiet and watch.
Tch.
I apologize, he’s just a hot-headed guy.
No, it’s fine, please go on.
Talk to the old man.
I really hate guys like you. But I hate that gloomy man who told me to investigate you even more.
Who in the world is that?
I can’t tell you that.
You seem suspicious.
I watched TV for the first time in a while, and it was such a depressing story.
It seems like I've been abandoned by Tsuki lately.
With Yuriko not here, I guess I should reflect on the past.
First, there’s the issue with Meryl.
As for Cindy, Tim’s audition should be over by now, so we need to get a conclusion soon.
If I drag this out any longer, Meryl will get mad.
Next, there’s the search for Ginny.
Even though Gabriela canceled, I’m still concerned about the scalpel Tonny dropped.
Though I don’t think the scalpel is a weapon, I won’t be able to draw any conclusions unless I investigate.
I wonder if the chemicals I asked Ash for have still not been obtained?
The owner of that scalpel and Ginny's last customer, Tonny Ford.
He even went into debt for Ginny, so his feelings for her must have been quite strong.
He said there was a rivalry over the director among Jennifer, Meryl, and Ginny at the Kasbah, but is that true?
The rumored Director Fabar who is liked by three women.
The director denied what Tonny was saying, but the truth is unclear.
Also, from her attitude when she showed the ring and cared about Ginny, I suspect she might be hiding something.
In the end, it turned out that Eva was really murdered.
It seemed she had grasped something about the serial murder case.
Besides, if her torso is missing,
the possibility that she came into contact with the killer and was killed is quite strong.
So, was Ginny also killed at the hands of that infamous serial killer?
Or was it a murder committed by a jealous Tonny?
Speaking of serial murder cases, some people said that Jennifer might be the murderer.
Gabriela said she holds a grudge against Ginny.
Is Jennifer the one behind all the murders?
By the way, Yuriko usually brings me the news, but she hasn't shown up today.
I wonder if she's feeling unwell?
Knock knock.
I wonder who is knocking on the door?
The door is open.
An elderly man.
Excuse me.
A young man.
Hmph.
An elderly man and a young man arrived. Both were unfamiliar and had the distinct vibe of police officers.
You two are,
Ah, I'm sorry for bothering you so early. I'm with the NYPD's Special Homicide Unit.
This is my partner,
Hey, why bother with this guy? Just rough him up a bit and get him to talk.
Come on, that's rude to Mr. Snipes.
It's true. He has a face like a fox and seems like a shady guy.
I understand you two are detectives, but what do you want with me?
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
